chunks.text: The court system of modern society is a way to settle conflict through law
When an incident occurs, the police first begin an investigation and arrest a suspect.
Then the prosecution gathers evidence and decides whether to bring charges.
When the trial begins, the prosecution and the defense each present their arguments.
Witness are called to the court and, after taking an oath, give their testimony.
The defendant also has the right to defend himself with the help of an attorney.
In many countries, a trial is presided over by a judge.
In serious criminal cases, some countries use a jury of citizens to decide the facts.
" A person is presumed innocent until guilt is proven.
This is one of the most important principles of modern law.
If the evidence is not sufficient, the defendant is found not guilty.
If the verdict is guilty, a sentence is given.
The punishment may be prison time, a fine, or community service.
If the defendant disagrees with the judgment, he can appeal to a higher court.
The system of justice must be fair to both victim and offender.
To realize the ideal of justice
humanity has spent a long history refining the system of the court.
